messages.author: S7uart G5AIL
messages.text: Hi chaps. Just thought I’d introduce myself. I’m a Uk ham, I’ve tried to build some free tools that you can use to help establish or strengthen your mesh. The site is called Meshrank.net. I’m actually looking for someone who would like to act as an observer in this area. If you setup an Observer node you also get access to some private functions like a Live map. Hope it’s of interest to You. It’s very much work in progress. MeshRank.net
messages.content_html: Hi chaps. Just thought I’d introduce myself. I’m a Uk ham, I’ve tried to build some free tools that you can use to help establish or strengthen your mesh. The site is called Meshrank.net. I’m actually looking for someone who would like to act as an observer in this area. If you setup an Observer node you also get access to some private functions like a Live map. Hope it’s of interest to You. It’s very much work in progress. MeshRank.net

messages.author: G3LJL PR5
messages.text: Hi, when this V4 arrives, I need to restore the keys from the V3, I am assuming I can use this tool https://map.mc868.hu/config/repeater-setup.html to save the config from the existing V3 and save to the V4??
messages.content_html: Hi, when this V4 arrives, I need to restore the keys from the V3, I am assuming I can use this tool https://map.mc868.hu/config/repeater-setup.html to save the config from the existing V3 and save to the V4??

messages.author: Tharsis
messages.text: If you plug your v3 into your PC and go to the Meshcore flasher page, then click the Console button next to repeater setup and connect to your repeater, then run get prv.key That'll return your private key. Copy and save it somewhere. Plug in your v4, flash it fully, then open the console set prv.key <The key you copied> And you'll be (mostly) golden. You'll need to manually set the name and radio settings again, but you can do that from the normal repeater setup tool also on the flasher page.
messages.content_html: If you plug your v3 into your PC and go to the Meshcore flasher page, then click the Console button next to repeater setup and connect to your repeater, then run get prv.key That'll return your private key. Copy and save it somewhere. Plug in your v4, flash it fully, then open the console set prv.key <The key you copied> And you'll be (mostly) golden. You'll need to manually set the name and radio settings again, but you can do that from the normal repeater setup tool also on the flasher page.
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: G3LJL PR5
messages.text: ahh fantastic, so that will restore the same public key too? should be here in a day or two although my V3 appears to be working much better now, Managed to get a friend on down in Telford and its working
messages.content_html: ahh fantastic, so that will restore the same public key too? should be here in a day or two although my V3 appears to be working much better now, Managed to get a friend on down in Telford and its working

messages.author: Tharsis
messages.text: Yeah, the public key is calculated from the private key, so as far as other nodes will see, it's the same device (Assuming you set the display name the same too).
messages.content_html: Yeah, the public key is calculated from the private key, so as far as other nodes will see, it's the same device (Assuming you set the display name the same too).
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: rogerken1
messages.text: Morning all. Haven’t been on here for a while. I’m at Lostock Hall. Set up a couple of repeaters (RKN) using V4’s ; one an omni ant and the other a yagi pointing towards Rivington. Mixed results so far.
messages.content_html: Morning all. Haven’t been on here for a while. I’m at Lostock Hall. Set up a couple of repeaters (RKN) using V4’s ; one an omni ant and the other a yagi pointing towards Rivington. Mixed results so far.

messages.author: G3LJL PR5
messages.text: I tried it on mine, marginal noise floor changes, maybe these fixes in next fw release if not already, im not familiar with how these releases work or whether inserted into next release or whether the link above has already been implemented. Am home tomorrow, so rptr been on a few days without monitoring so be good to see if any improvement in rx

messages.author: S7uart G5AIL
messages.text: I’ve started to see some of the repeaters in the live MeshMap move to multibyte. I can’t work out if it’s a particular area testing or if they’ve just jumped in. Notice the purple vs blue. I do wonder if this is part of the reason (edited)
